message:
beads: advance ft-xbnl0.2.4 + reopen wa-283h4.4 with blocker note

Two tracker flips from the Asupersync / io_uring work:

  - ft-xbnl0.2.4  Replace TCP/TLS/HTTP client + service boundaries
                  with native Asupersync networking.
                  open -> in_progress. SunnyTiger took the issue;
                  the goal-line sequence now has an active owner.

  - wa-283h4.4    io_uring zero-copy mux I/O pipeline.
                  in_progress -> open, with a new comment (id 3591)
                  surfacing the implementation blocker:

                  "Verified seam is
                   crates/frankenterm-mux-server-impl/src/dispatch.rs
                   and non-overlap with ft-2h5wv (mux_pool.rs).
                   Current `io-uring` feature only flips
                   DispatchIoBackend::current_default() in
                   dispatch.rs plus a platform-default test; there
                   is no existing ring-backed read/write path. A
                   real Linux fast path here requires either
                   (a) adding a new direct dependency/wrapper, or
                   (b) allowing unsafe in frankenterm-mux-server-impl
                   and hand-rolling io_uring setup / mmap /
                   register / submit / complete there. Also,
                   TLS AsyncSslStream cannot use the kernel zero-copy
                   path, so the design has to split UnixStream-only
                   ring I/O from TLS/std fallback. Reopening instead
                   of leaving this falsely in_progress."

The reopen is deliberate: the prior in_progress state implied
active forward motion, which isn't accurate until the design split
(UnixStream ring path vs. TLS/std fallback) is agreed and either a
crate dep or a vetted `unsafe` block is authorized.

No code changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
